suggestions.label: Transformer basics
suggestions.text: Explain the Transformer architecture: attention mechanisms, encoders, and decoders.

suggestions.label: Attention mechanism
suggestions.text: How does self-attention work in Transformers? Explain queries, keys, and values.

suggestions.label: Tokenisation
suggestions.text: Explain tokenisation methods: BPE, WordPiece, and SentencePiece with examples.

suggestions.label: Pre-training
suggestions.text: How are LLMs pre-trained? Explain causal language modelling and masked LM objectives.

suggestions.label: Fine-tuning
suggestions.text: What is fine-tuning? Explain full fine-tuning, LoRA, and instruction tuning.

suggestions.label: Prompt engineering
suggestions.text: Teach me prompt engineering techniques: zero-shot, few-shot, chain-of-thought, and ReAct.

suggestions.label: RLHF
suggestions.text: Explain Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) and how it aligns LLMs.

suggestions.label: RAG
suggestions.text: What is Retrieval-Augmented Generation? How does it work and when should I use it?

suggestions.label: LLM evaluation
suggestions.text: How do you evaluate LLM performance? Explain benchmarks, metrics, and evaluation challenges.

suggestions.label: Inference optimisation
suggestions.text: Explain LLM inference optimisation: quantisation, KV cache, speculative decoding.

suggestions.label: Safety and alignment
suggestions.text: What are the main LLM safety challenges? Explain hallucination, bias, and jailbreaking.

suggestions.label: LLM agents
suggestions.text: What are LLM agents? Explain tool use, function calling, and multi-agent systems.

suggestions.label: Scaling laws
suggestions.text: Explain LLM scaling laws: how do model size, data, and compute relate to performance?

suggestions.label: Multimodal LLMs
suggestions.text: How do multimodal LLMs work? Explain vision-language models and audio-text models.

suggestions.label: Practice
suggestions.text: Give me a challenging LLM engineering problem to work through.
